question_text: Why do we see different phases of the Moon during the month?

options.label: A
options.text: The Moon produces different amounts of light each night

options.label: B
options.text: Earth's shadow covers different amounts of the Moon as it orbits

options.label: C
options.text: We see different amounts of the illuminated side of the Moon as it orbits Earth

options.label: D
options.text: The Moon moves closer and farther from the Sun

explanation: As the Moon orbits Earth, the Sun illuminates different amounts of its surface. From our viewpoint, we see the Moon's phases change from new (fully dark) to full (fully lit) and back again, depending on the Moon's position in its orbit.
